evals.name: Phase Selection — Always Ask
assertions: MUST ask user which phase to run (Evergreen, Timely, or Both) before starting any research
MUST NOT auto-select a phase or start scraping without user confirmation
MUST accept inline phase specification like '/yt-research evergreen cold email' without re-asking

evals.name: Keyword Discovery — Autocomplete Scraper
assertions: MUST use Apify actor scraper-mind/youtube-autocomplete-scraper for keyword discovery
MUST call fetch-actor-details before call-actor to verify input schema
MUST generate 20-50 candidate keywords from the seed topic
MUST deduplicate keywords (case-insensitive)
MUST NOT use WebSearch for keyword discovery

evals.name: Real Search Volume — Google Keyword Planner
assertions: MUST use Apify actor iskander/google-keyword-search-volume-api to get real search volume data
MUST call fetch-actor-details before call-actor to verify input schema
MUST pass discovered keywords as a batch to the actor
MUST report actual monthly search volume numbers in the final report (not just relative scores)
MUST NOT use WebSearch or Google Trends as a substitute for real volume data
MUST NOT skip this step or use only autocomplete position as a volume proxy

evals.name: Competition Scoring — YouTube Video Analysis
assertions: MUST use Apify streamers/youtube-scraper to scrape top 3 videos per keyword
MUST calculate saturation (% with keyword in title)
MUST calculate channel_barrier (avg subscriber count of top channels)
MUST calculate freshness_gap (avg days since upload — 12+ months = opportunity)
MUST score competition 1-5 with evidence from the scraped data

evals.name: Authority Scoring — YouTube Analytics MCP
assertions: MUST attempt to use YouTube Analytics MCP tools (get_channel_videos, get_search_terms, get_engagement_metrics)
MUST score ranking probability 1-5 based on Jay's existing content, search terms, and engagement
MUST provide evidence for each ranking score (e.g., 'Jay has 3 related videos with 2.5% engagement')
If YouTube Analytics MCP unavailable, MUST warn user and use fallback score of 3
MUST NOT skip the ranking factor entirely

evals.name: Unicorn Detection — Competitor Outliers
assertions: MUST load competitor list from youtube-competitors.json reference file
MUST NOT hardcode competitor handles or URLs
MUST scrape top 10 videos per competitor channel via streamers/youtube-scraper
MUST scrape ALL competitors in the relevant pillar(s), not just 2-3
MUST calculate outlier_score as views / median_channel_views
MUST filter to only videos with outlier_score >= 10 (10x+ their average)
MUST NOT surface videos below 10x as unicorns

evals.name: Unicorn Analysis — Hook and Angle Extraction
assertions: For each unicorn video, MUST extract: title, topic, view count, outlier_score, upload date
MUST classify each unicorn's content angle (tutorial, reaction, comparison, news, hot_take, case_study, tool_demo, listicle, prediction)
MUST suggest how Jay could cover the unicorn topic with a different or better angle
MUST score unicorn topics on the same 4-factor matrix as keywords

evals.name: Scoring Matrix — Correct Weights Per Phase
assertions: Evergreen phase MUST use weights: Opportunity 35%, Ranking 30%, Trend 15%, Content Fit 20%
Timely phase MUST use weights: Opportunity 25%, Ranking 20%, Trend 35%, Content Fit 20%
MUST NOT use the same weights for both phases
Each idea MUST have all 4 factors scored individually with evidence
MUST NOT assign batch-identical scores to all ideas (each must have unique factor assessments)
MUST classify ideas into MAKE IT (16-20), WATCH (12-15), or SKIP (0-11)
Final scores MUST be on the 0-20 scale

evals.name: Timely Phase — Velocity Detection
assertions: MUST scrape recent videos (last 7 days) from ALL competitors in youtube-competitors.json
MUST calculate velocity_score = views_per_day / channel_avg_views_per_day
MUST filter to velocity >= 5.0 for MAKE IT candidates
MUST filter to velocity >= 3.0 for WATCH candidates
MUST NOT flag normal-performing videos (velocity < 1.5) as trending
MUST show velocity multiplier in the report (e.g., '8.2x channel average')

evals.name: Timely Phase — Content-Research Bridge
assertions: MUST query Supabase scraped_ideas table for ideas from the last 3 days
If recent ideas found: MUST surface top 5 by relevance_score with suggested YouTube long-form angles
If no recent ideas found: MUST explicitly offer to run /content-research trending
MUST NOT silently skip the content-research bridge step
MUST NOT use WebSearch as a substitute for the Supabase query

evals.name: Pre-Flight — Graceful Degradation
assertions: MUST verify Apify MCP is accessible before starting
MUST check if YouTube Analytics MCP tools are available
If YouTube Analytics MCP unavailable: MUST warn user with clear message
If YouTube Analytics MCP unavailable: MUST use fallback ranking score of 3 for all keywords
If YouTube Analytics MCP unavailable: MUST NOT fail or skip Phase 1 entirely
MUST load reference files (youtube-competitors.json, yt-scoring-matrices.json) before scoring

evals.name: Report Format — Structured Output
assertions: Report MUST include a table with columns: Keyword/Topic, Opportunity, Ranking, Trend, Fit, Score
Each MAKE IT item MUST include: why-it-scores-high explanation, search volume, competition gap, Jay's advantage, suggested angle
MAKE IT items MUST include next step: 'Run /youtube-script [keyword]'
Report MUST separate MAKE IT, WATCH, and SKIP tiers
Unicorn section MUST be separate from keyword section
Report MUST include summary with counts and top recommendation

evals.name: Save Behavior — Correct Tables
assertions: Keywords scoring 60+ MUST be saved to yt_longtail_keywords table via POST /api/ideas/yt-longtail
Unicorn outliers MUST be saved to outlier_ideas table with research_context = 'yt-research-unicorn'
Saved keywords MUST include: keyword, seed_topic, volume_score, competition_score, opportunity_score, trend, category, suggested_angles
Category assignment MUST follow: 80+ = quick_win, 60-79 = growth_bet, 40-59 = standard, <40 = avoid

evals.name: Competitor List — Reference File Usage
assertions: MUST read youtube-competitors.json from .claude/skills/yt-research/references/
MUST NOT hardcode competitor channel handles or URLs in the skill execution
MUST use all competitors from the relevant pillar based on seed topic
If topic spans multiple pillars, MUST include competitors from all relevant pillars
